rationale: The confirmed US-Iran ceasefire and Hormuz reopening is the most impactful new development for Romania, potentially easing energy costs; Black Sea security context remains unchanged.

top_events.title: US, Iran announce ceasefire agreement; Strait of Hormuz reopening
top_events.summary: Confirmed ceasefire and Hormuz reopening could reduce global energy prices, benefiting Romanian households and industry still exposed to elevated energy costs. A qualitative new development relative to prior unresolved tensions.

top_events.title: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in Geneva
top_events.summary: G7 summit protests are distant from Romanian daily life; no direct security or economic impact on Romania.

top_events.title: Russia-Ukraine war continues
top_events.summary: No qualitative escalation near Romania's border this cycle; Black Sea security situation unchanged.
